name: Jess's Maternity Swim
description: 7 swim workouts to rotate through whenever — no fixed schedule, just pick whatever sounds fun today. Designed for a former high school swimmer in the 2nd trimester. No butterfly, no flip turns, no diving. Pool temp 78-84°F.
creator: WOD

schedule.label: Drill Day
schedule.notes: All drills, all technique. Get out swimming better than you got in.

schedule.label: Kick Party
schedule.notes: Legs, hips, glutes. Breaststroke kick = free hip mobility work.

schedule.label: Pull & Chill
schedule.notes: Upper body with pull buoy. Legs rest after kick day.

schedule.label: Mix Tape
schedule.notes: A little of everything. The 'never bored' workout.

schedule.label: Long & Easy
schedule.notes: Steady-state, zone out, rack up yards. The meditative one.

schedule.label: Fast & Short
schedule.notes: Short intervals with lots of rest. Scratches the competitive itch.

schedule.label: Recovery & Play
schedule.notes: Sculling, treading, pool walking. The lightest option.

tips: NEVER: butterfly, flip turns, diving, hot tubs (>90°F)
ALWAYS: open turns, water bottle on deck, breathe as often as you need
No schedule — just pick whatever sounds fun and go swim
Swimming is one of the best exercises during pregnancy — buoyancy supports the belly, zero impact on joints
If something feels off, dial it back or get out. Trust your body.
As the belly grows, breaststroke and backstroke may feel better than freestyle — lean into it
Pull buoy becomes more comfortable as pregnancy progresses — the extra buoyancy is your friend
Stay hydrated even though you're in water — you're still sweating
Consult your OB before starting — swimming is generally very safe but always check first

overview.duration: Ongoing through 2nd trimester (adjust as needed into 3rd)
overview.goal: Stay active, have fun in the water, maintain fitness during pregnancy
overview.whoIsItFor: Former competitive swimmer who wants varied pool workouts during pregnancy
overview.whatToExpect: 7 workouts to pick from, 25-35 min each, 800-2000y. Pick whatever sounds fun — no order, no pressure.
keyPrinciples: No schedule — just a menu of workouts to choose from
No butterfly — too much back extension and abdominal pressure
No flip turns — open turns only
Effort stays conversational with brief moderate pushes on the intervals workout
Breaststroke kick doubles as hip mobility work
Pull buoy = extra buoyancy as belly grows
Listen to your body above all else

avoid: Butterfly stroke — excessive back extension and abdominal pressure
Hot pools/hot tubs — water temp should be 78-84°F
All-out sprints — keep max effort around 80%
Swimming alone — always have someone aware you're in the pool
encouraged: Backstroke — neutral spine, face out of water
Freestyle — great cardio, modify breathing as needed
Kick work — legs and hips without impact
Pull buoy — extra support as belly grows
Pool walking — zero impact, water supports 90% of body weight
pregnancySafety.warning: Always consult your healthcare provider before starting an exercise program during pregnancy. Stop immediately if you experience dizziness, bleeding, contractions, chest pain, or anything that doesn't feel right.
